formulas.name: First Normal Form (1NF)
formulas.variables: A table is in 1NF if each cell contains a single value.
formulas.notes: A table should not have repeating groups of columns.

formulas.name: Second Normal Form (2NF)
formulas.variables: A table is in 2NF if it is in 1NF and all non-key columns depend on the entire primary key.
formulas.notes: If a table has a composite primary key, it should be in 2NF.

formulas.name: Third Normal Form (3NF)
formulas.variables: A table is in 3NF if it is in 2NF and there are no transitive dependencies.
formulas.notes: If a table has a transitive dependency, it should be moved to a new table.

formulas.name: Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)
formulas.variables: A table is in BCNF if it is in 3NF and there are no transitive dependencies.
formulas.notes: BCNF is a stronger form of 3NF.

formulas.name: Entity Relationship (ER) Model
formulas.variables: The ER model is a visual representation of entities and their relationships.
formulas.notes: The ER model is used to design databases.
